# REQ-CR-260008: Stack Rejection and Scope Constraints for MVP

- Class: CR
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-04-17
- Source decision: H6 (Rejection criteria)

## Requirement

MVP delivery shall reject stack choices that force SSR-first workspace behavior, erode feature-boundary governance, provide weak TypeScript/GraphQL ecosystem support, or require heavy custom glue to deliver core graph workflows.

## Rationale

Explicit rejection constraints protect delivery focus and reduce architectural risk from incompatible stack patterns.

## Scope Notes

- In scope for MVP constraint: stack screening and exclusion rules before implementation commitments.
- Out of scope for this requirement: post-MVP technology experimentation outside core workspace runtime.

## Verification

- Stack review checks confirm selected technologies satisfy rejection constraints.
- Architecture checks confirm no forced SSR-first workspace behavior is introduced.
- Dependency checks confirm ecosystem/tooling maturity remains acceptable for MVP goals.

## Traceability

- Related ADRs: ADR-260019
- Related backlog items: BI-260016
